snippet.title: Learn from the outliers: Phebe Novakovic
snippet.description:
Three main reasons why transparency is important for leaders:

Builds Trust: Transparency helps to establish trust between leaders and their team members. When leaders are transparent about their decision-making processes, it shows that they have nothing to hide and are willing to be held accountable for their actions. This trust can lead to increased collaboration, better communication, and higher morale among team members.

Fosters Collaboration: Transparency promotes collaboration by creating an environment where everyone feels comfortable sharing their ideas and opinions. When leaders are transparent about their plans and strategies, it encourages team members to speak up and offer suggestions, leading to more innovative solutions and better decision-making.

Encourages Accountability: Transparency holds leaders accountable for their actions and decisions. When leaders are transparent, they are more likely to take responsibility for their mistakes and learn from them. This accountability can also extend to other team members, who will feel more inclined to own up to their errors and work towards improvement. Ultimately, this leads to a culture of continuous learning and growth.
